title: Provision Xcode team/profile for studio WDA builds
description: Studio deploy is ready and the WDA project path is valid, but manual xcodebuild for WebDriverAgentRunner fails with: No Account for Team QMD6A5SXQ9, no matching signing certificate/private key, and no provisioning profile for com.facebook.WebDriverAgentRunner.xctrunner. Until Xcode account/team provisioning is configured on the studio, WDA cannot launch and TikTok warm-up cannot run.

title: Scheduler refactor: role-aware dispatch

title: Device-only clean room architecture
description: Enforce all persona-facing traffic through physical iPhones over cellular. 10 phones: 2 seeders (rotating every 4-6h), 8 warmers (7-14 day cycle). Mac Studio orchestrates via WDA only. Never makes persona-facing HTTP requests.
close_reason: Epic complete: all child tasks implemented

title: Use prebuilt WDA runner mode in studio launch agents
description: On the Mac Studio, the generated/loaded WDA launch agents still run xcodebuild test, which re-enters signing inside the launchctl context and fails with No Account / missing profile even after WebDriverAgentRunner has been built successfully in Xcode. The stable runtime path is xcodebuild test-without-building with per-device DerivedData paths. Update the launch-agent generation/runtime setup to use the prebuilt runner mode so WDA survives scheduler restarts without manual intervention.
close_reason: Switched generated WDA launch agents to Appium 3 prebuilt test-without-building mode with per-device DerivedData and verified both phones on studio launchctl path

title: Exclude failed warm sessions from daily cap
description: Identity guard daily cap currently counts all session_log rows started today, including failed, aborted, and skipped sessions. On the studio this leaves otherwise healthy devices blocked after error-heavy warm-up runs. The cap should only count non-error completed sessions so transient failures do not consume the daily quota.
close_reason: Count only completed successful sessions toward the identity-guard daily cap; verified live on studio with iPhone-B resuming work despite 24 failed sessions

title: Treat busy WDA as busy instead of disconnected
description: Observed during live studio warm-up on March 16, 2026: scheduler _wait_for_wda collapses httpx.ReadTimeout from GET /status into a hard disconnect even while the WDA xcodebuild runner is still processing long TikTok UI commands. This causes repeated false device_disconnected events and status flips during live sessions. Fix scheduler and legacy manual path to distinguish busy from unreachable, and add regression coverage.
close_reason: Classified busy WDA timeouts separately from unreachable WDA and added regression coverage

title: Bootstrap device roles safely for small fleets
description: Current bootstrap logic uses a fixed SEEDER_COUNT=2. On small fleets this can allocate all active devices as seeders and leave zero warmers, which is incompatible with TikTok warm-up operations. Need role bootstrap/rotation logic to guarantee at least one warmer when the fleet is smaller than the nominal target size.

title: Prevent multiple concurrent scheduler start processes on studio
description: Observed on the Mac Studio on March 16, 2026: repeated  invocations left orphaned scheduler processes running simultaneously. This caused duplicate device loops, repeated pre-session checks, and conflicting WDA traffic on the same phones. Need a single-instance guard or PID/lock enforcement for scheduler start and clearer stop/status semantics.

title: Fix generated WDA launchd plists for Appium 3 studio installs
description: generate_launchd_plists() hardcodes /opt/homebrew/bin/xcodebuild and the old Appium 2 WebDriverAgent path under /opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/appium/node_modules/appium-webdriveragent. On the studio, the valid paths are /usr/bin/xcodebuild and ~/.appium/node_modules/appium-xcuitest-driver/node_modules/appium-webdriveragent/WebDriverAgent.xcodeproj. Generated plists currently fail before WDA can start.

title: Validate warm-up process on studio after lifecycle hardening
description: Deploy the warm-up lifecycle hardening to the studio host, run the production warm-up path there, monitor the scheduler/device behavior, and capture any runtime failures or follow-up work.

title: Dispatch persona/email tasks claimed by warmer loop
description: DeviceScheduler._get_next_task can return create_persona_account and create_email, but _run_warmer_iteration only dispatches warm/create. Warmers currently claim those tasks, mark the session failed/skipped, and waste device time instead of executing or rejecting the task type. Decide whether warmers should execute these task types or stop claiming them, then add regression coverage.
close_reason: Dispatched persona and email creation tasks from warmer loop with regression coverage

title: Stabilize Mac Studio TikTok warm-up pipeline
description: Review the warm-up/account automation pipeline used on the Mac Studio for TikTok accounts, identify reliability failures, implement fixes, add tests, and document follow-up work discovered during the review.

title: Clean-room enforcement layer
description: Import guard (_clean_room.py) and network guard (_network_guard.py) in device/. HTTP allowlist for WDA localhost + infrastructure APIs only. CI pre-commit check for violations.
close_reason: All implemented: quarantine, migration, roles, seeder_email, email_reader, clean_room, scheduler refactor, distribution handoff

title: Replace legacy warm commands with account-aware manual runner
description: The legacy warm_all/runner entry points now fail fast unless --allow-existing-session is passed because they bypass delete/install/login. Remaining work: add an account-aware manual warm command for operator use, or remove the legacy path entirely once scheduler-based warming is the only supported production path.

title: Distribution handoff process
description: When account reaches warming_day_count>=14 and state=active: set distribution_eligible_at, unbind from device. Query for distribution-ready accounts.
close_reason: All implemented: quarantine, migration, roles, seeder_email, email_reader, clean_room, scheduler refactor, distribution handoff
